intitule: Vocabulary Review: Social Issues, Work, Health & Environment

enonce.texte:
Associez chaque mot ou expression à sa définition. Écrivez la lettre correspondante.

  1. volunteer
  2. erosion
  3. vaccine
  4. qualifications
  5. recycle
  6. abuse of rights

Définitions :
A. The process of wearing away soil by wind or water.
B. A substance given to prevent a disease.
C. Skills and experience needed for a job.
D. Someone who works without pay to help others.
E. Convert waste into reusable material.
F. Violation of a person's legal or human rights.

questions.texte: Associez chaque mot de la liste à la définition correcte.

intitule: Grammar Review: Passive Voice, Reported Speech, Conditionals

questions.texte: Mettez la phrase suivante à la voix passive : "The government questioned the educational system."
questions.reponse: The educational system was questioned (by the government).

questions.texte: Transformez ce discours direct en discours indirect : "The Mayor said, 'We will sort out our garbage.'"
questions.reponse: The Mayor announced that they would sort out their garbage.

questions.texte: Complétez la phrase conditionnelle (Type 1) : "If you ______ (take) the vaccine, you ______ (be) better protected."

enonce.texte:
Lisez le témoignage suivant :

"In my community, many girls between 12 and 15 are taken out of school. They are forced to marry older men. Their parents receive money. The girls lose their chance to become nurses or teachers."

questions.texte: Identifiez deux problèmes sociaux décrits dans ce texte.
questions.reponse: Child marriage and denial of education for girls.

questions.texte: Proposez une solution concrète que le comité de parents ou les élèves pourraient mettre en place pour lutter contre ce problème.
questions.reponse: Organiser des campagnes de sensibilisation sur les droits des enfants, ou créer un programme de bourses pour garder les filles à l'école.

intitule: Applying for a Job: Cover Letter Analysis

enonce.texte:
Voici une annonce d'emploi :

Position: Community Health Assistant
Requirements: High school certificate, ability to speak English and French, experience in volunteering preferred.
Salary: Competitive.

Vous devez écrire une lettre de motivation en utilisant le present perfect pour parler de vos expériences et des modaux d'obligation (have to) ou de conseil (should).

questions.texte: Rédigez une lettre de motivation d'environ 100 mots. Mentionnez : (a) vos expériences pertinentes (present perfect), (b) pourquoi vous êtes obligé(e) de travailler (have to), (c) un conseil pour la communauté (should).
questions.reponse: Exemple de réponse : Dear Hiring Manager, I have volunteered at the local clinic for two years. I have also helped during polio vaccination campaigns. I have to work because I want to support my family and gain experience. The community should practice better hygiene to prevent diseases. I believe my skills match your requirements. Sincerely, [Name]

enonce.texte:
Imaginez que vous êtes le délégué de votre classe. Vous devez faire un discours de 2 minutes pour sensibiliser vos camarades sur l'érosion des sols.

Utilisez :
- Le present continuous pour décrire le processus ($Soil is eroding...$)
- Le gérondif comme sujet ($Recycling helps...$)
- Le causatif have/get ($We should have our garbage collected...$)
- Des mots de liaison (because, therefore, however, although).
images: Image d'un terrain en pente avec une couche de terre qui s'écoule, arbres déracinés, et une petite rivière boueuse.

questions.texte: Rédigez le texte intégral de votre discours. Mettez en évidence les causes, les conséquences et trois solutions concrètes.
questions.reponse: Exemple de réponse : Dear fellow students, Soil is eroding faster than it is being formed in our village. Therefore, our crops are failing. Recycling plant matter into compost helps rebuild topsoil. However, many people ignore this. Because of erosion, we risk losing our farmland. We should have our waste sorted regularly. Although it requires effort, planting trees and building windbreaks can stop erosion. Let's act now!
